steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice B
steps.content: Choice B claims the team moves about 4.8 tons per hour. In the linear model $$y = 120 - 25x$$, the slope $$-25$$ indicates the cargo is decreasing at 25 tons per hour. Since 4.8 ≠ 25, choice B does not describe the x‑intercept.

steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice C
steps.content: Choice C states the team moves about 25 tons per hour. While the slope magnitude is 25 tons/hour, the x‑intercept represents a time, not a rate. Therefore choice C does not interpret the x‑intercept.

steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice A
steps.content: To find the x‑intercept set $$y = 0$$: $$0 = 120 - 25x$$ $$→$$ $$25x = 120$$ $$→$$ $$x = \frac{120}{25} = 4.8$$. This means after 4.8 hours no cargo remains, i.e., the team has finished moving all the cargo. That matches choice A — A is correct.
